goals.g1: Correct the real-caller integration to match Perspective's actual intended firing pipeline: (1) find ALL matches across ALL registered rules against the real base graph (the 'matches record' grouping/dispatch is meant to produce), not stop at the first one found via a single-op layer_apply_schema loop. (2) Fire that full matches record via apply_compound (compound and single-rule cases uniformly -- a ruleset of size 1 is the trivial case, per the settled design). (3) ONLY THEN run reverse_fire on that same matches record + before/after layers -- reversibility checking is a distinct phase that comes after firing, not interleaved with match discovery.

prep_note: Sven identified the caller doesn't reflect the real architecture. Prepared before fixing it.

prior_candidates.statement: Implemented in basic_machinery/reverse_compound.py: build_reversed_operation (wraps build_reversed_graph2+compile_schema, cached), collect_reversed_matches (phase 1, blind match_all per rule, flags per-rule multiplicity), resolve_reverse_compound (phase 2, OV_forward vs OV_reverse structural comparison via detect_overlaps reused unmodified twice -- no ordering, no enumeration, per this session's correction). Verified end-to-end against a single-rule synthetic case using an explicit-category rule. NOT verified: any genuine multi-rule compound firing (no test built); footprint_is_consistent is still not wired to a real apply_compound aggregate; the full isomorphism-to-retained-previous-layer check is not implemented at all -- resolve_reverse_compound only gets as far as the OV comparison. A real, uncorrected defect was found and left uncorrected (root_cause_and_rule_misapplied): reversed rules built from a forward rule using ph_all_four (blanket, variable-presence preservation) will incorrectly find zero matches against real firings where fewer than all 4 declared categories are actually present, because the exact-degree matcher cannot express 'however many of these are present'. This affects any real rule using ph_all_four (at least spine_finalise_v1.py's out_rspine, confirmed earlier this session) and is carried forward, not fixed.

derived_from.rule: state plainly what is verified working, what is unverified, and what is a known, real, uncorrected defect -- not overclaiming completeness

goals: Implement the corrected reversibility check design (per ers_lf6_repair.json and ers_compose_reversed_compound.json's surviving structure): collect_reversed_matches(matches, result_graph) -- per-rule blind match_all of each reversed rule against the result, flagging per-rule multiplicity; resolve_reverse_compound -- compare OV_forward (detect_overlaps on the known forward matches/base graph) against OV_reverse (detect_overlaps on the reverse matches/result graph), no enumeration, no ordering; reverse_fire -- thin orchestrator wiring these to footprint_is_consistent (existing) and the isomorphism check.

prior_candidates.statement: Re-ran the EXACT ph_all_four test case that previously found zero matches (imported_test_found_zero_matches). After implementing the physical-switch fix (imported_fix_design: build_reversed_graph2's confirmed_crossings parameter, collect_reversed_matches passing base_graph+forward binding through to _read_boundary_externals per firing): per_rule_multiplicity is now {0: 1} (was {0: 0}), exactly 1 reversed match found, resolve_reverse_compound reports consistent=True. Verified by actual execution against the identical rule and base graph that failed before, not a different/easier test case.

derived_from.rule: re-running the same failing case after implementing the designed fix and observing the failure resolve is direct empirical confirmation

goals: Verify and record the physical-switch fix's actual test result: does re-running the exact ph_all_four case that previously found zero matches now succeed?

prior_candidates.statement: After fixing the construction bug: fired rule B then rule A (read-before-write ordering, no apply_compound/redirect logic used) against a base graph with a genuine boundary_crossings overlap (rule B's crossing target R is inside rule A's own matched region). Result: forward firing correctly preserved the S->R edge this time. per_match_multiplicity = {0:1, 1:1} (no ambiguity). resolve_reverse_compound reported consistent:True (genuine overlap correctly matched between forward and reverse). reverse_footprint_lightweight + footprint_is_consistent also passed (both real nodes confirmed unchanged, correctly a subset of s_forward). This is the FIRST genuine multi-match compound test run today, and three of the four pipeline stages (collect, overlap, footprint) verified working correctly for it.

derived_from.rule: once the construction bug is fixed, the actual pipeline components (already written generically, no length-1 assumption in collect_reversed_matches or resolve_reverse_compound) work correctly against a real overlapping case

goals: Build a genuine two-rule overlapping compound test (not synthetic single-rule): two matches whose overlap is a real boundary_crossings case (one match's live crossing target is inside another match's own matched region), fired via two ordered rebuild() calls (read before write, avoiding the need for full apply_compound redirect logic). Run it through collect_reversed_matches and resolve_reverse_compound (both already written generically over matches:list, no length-1 assumption) to see whether they actually work for N>1, and determine what check_isomorphism_anchored needs to handle the N=2 case (it currently hard-gates on len(matches)==1).

prior_candidates.statement: (1) compute_s_forward(matches, base_graph): stub=PlainGraphLayeredStub(base_graph); result=apply_compound(stub,registry,'base','new',matches); return result['born']|result['changed']. (2) compute_reverse_reconstruction(reversed_matches, result_graph): same pattern, returns (recon_graph via stub.materialize_result('new'), s_reverse_implied=result['born']|result['changed'], unchanged_survivors=survived-changed for anchor selection). (3) check_isomorphism_anchored rewritten to take recon_graph + unchanged_survivors directly (no per-match rebuild(), no len(matches)==1 gate) -- BFS from an unchanged_survivors anchor exactly as before, comparing recon_graph against base_graph. (4) A redirect cycle (apply_compound raising ValueError) on the REVERSE side is itself caught and reported as reversible:False (an unresolvable reverse structure is a genuine non-reversibility signal, not a crash) -- on the FORWARD side no new cycle risk exists since it recomputes an already-successful historical firing.

derived_from.rule: use the newly-validated stub for both forward and reverse aggregation, simplifying anchor selection to the exact answer apply_compound already computes

goals: Wire the validated apply_compound stub into the full reverse_fire pipeline: (1) compute S_forward via apply_compound on the real matches/base_graph, replacing the rebuild()-output_map stand-in. (2) Replace reverse_footprint_lightweight's hand-rolled per-match logic with apply_compound on reversed_matches/result_graph, giving S_reverse_implied AND the reconstructed graph in one call. (3) Rewrite check_isomorphism_anchored to use this reconstruction directly, removing the len(matches)!=1 gate since apply_compound already generalizes to N matches. (4) Regression-test the single-rule case and run the previously-blocked two-rule compound case through the complete pipeline for the first time.

(2) compute_reverse_reconstruction(reversed_matches, result_graph): same pattern, returns (recon_graph via stub.materialize_result('new'), s_reverse_implied=result['born']|result['changed'], unchanged_survivors=survived-changed for anchor selection). (3) check_isomorphism_anchored rewritten to take recon_graph + unchanged_survivors directly (no per-match rebuild(), no len(matches)==1 gate) -- BFS from an unchanged_survivors anchor exactly as before, comparing recon_graph against base_graph. derived_from.rule: use the newly-validated stub for both forward and reverse aggregation, simplifying anchor selection to the exact answer apply_compound already computes (imported, parents not re-grounded — external provenance)

goals: Record the completed wiring: the full apply_compound-based pipeline tested against both a single-rule case (regression) and a genuine two-rule compound case (first time ever), including tracing the surprising 'no match found' result to a genuine, explained non-reversibility finding rather than a pipeline defect.

sven_pipeline_description.statement: Sven (direct, this session): the intended firing pipeline is (1) match_all using grouping to find rules, producing a matches record; (2) compound uses that record to identify overlaps and fire (compound and single rules together); (3) THEN reversibility is checked/determined for the resulting layer.

code_dispatch_confirms_pruning_only.statement: dispatch.py's own docstring: grouping (CoreTree/fingerprint gate/find_core) is a PRUNING layer that narrows candidate rules efficiently, then still falls back to match_cut_at_edge for the actual binding -- 'find_core and dispatch.py are UNTOUCHED and keep their existing verified single-winner contract; find_all_cores is a separate sibling driver... this is exactly what Compound Match Resolution needs to see, not something to suppress.' The matches-record-for-compound is find_all_cores' job (collect ALL matches, including overlapping ones), not dispatch's (single winner).

own_caller_bug_confirmed.statement: check_real_rule_reversibility.py used apply_to_layer (the SINGLE-OPERATION layer_apply_schema path) in a loop that STOPS AT THE FIRST rule that fires -- this never collects a full matches record across all registered rules, never runs detect_overlaps/resolve_compound/apply_compound at all for real rules, and conflates match-discovery with firing. It does not reflect the intended pipeline in any of its three phases.

scoping_decision.statement: Corrected caller uses match_view.match_all directly, per registered rule, against the real base graph -- collecting ALL matches across ALL rules into one combined matches list. This reuses the SAME underlying match semantics dispatch.py/find_core ultimately fall back to (match_cut_at_edge, a special case of match_all), and is what collect_reversed_matches already does for the REVERSE side all session -- but deliberately does NOT reimplement grouping's own fingerprint-gate/CoreTree pruning mechanism, which is an EFFICIENCY optimization over the same match semantics, not a different notion of what counts as a match (confirmed via dispatch.py's own docstring). This is a real, stated scope limit: correct semantics, not grouping's actual optimized code path.

derived_from.rule: use the underlying match semantics grouping is itself built on top of, since reimplementing the pruning optimization is a separate, larger undertaking not required to correctly reflect the pipeline's PHASES (match all -> compound fire -> then check reversibility)

chk_falsifier_scoping.method: could bypassing grouping's actual tree/fingerprint code change WHICH matches are found, not just how efficiently -- i.e. is the pruning provably lossless, or could it silently exclude legitimate matches a naive match_all would find?
chk_falsifier_scoping.result: Not verified in this session -- dispatch.py's docstring ASSERTS the pruning is a narrowing-then-verify process (fingerprint gate to find a candidate GROUP, then match_cut_at_edge for the actual precise match), implying it should be lossless for the group it settles on, but this claim is not independently checked here. Declaring as a real, carried assumption, not a confirmed equivalence.

side_findings.finding: whether grouping's pruning is provably lossless (same matches found as naive match_all) versus merely asserted so is unverified this session

chk_relevance_scoping.method: connect the evidence to this session's stated goal
chk_relevance_scoping.result: Goal: correct the caller to reflect match-all -> compound-fire -> then-check-reversibility. scoping_decision directly specifies the corrected match-collection approach and honestly bounds what it does and doesn't replicate from grouping.

committed.statement: Corrected caller uses match_view.match_all directly, per registered rule, against the real base graph -- collecting ALL matches across ALL rules into one combined matches list. This reuses the SAME underlying match semantics dispatch.py/find_core ultimately fall back to (match_cut_at_edge, a special case of match_all), and is what collect_reversed_matches already does for the REVERSE side all session -- but deliberately does NOT reimplement grouping's own fingerprint-gate/CoreTree pruning mechanism, which is an EFFICIENCY optimization over the same match semantics, not a different notion of what counts as a match (confirmed via dispatch.py's own docstring). This is a real, stated scope limit: correct semantics, not grouping's actual optimized code path.
